untranslatable_reason: Signal requires detection of 'artificial price ceiling legally enforced' and 'price ceiling removed' — these are external regulatory events/states that cannot be derived from price/volume indicators alone. The signal also requires knowledge of an absolute ceiling price level (e.g., $110 for plywood) which must be supplied externally as a parameter, not calculated from available indicators. Additionally, 'constraint creates predictable boundary' and 'supply/demand pressure cannot breach' are qualitative assessments requiring market microstructure data not available in the standard indicator set.
translation_notes: The core trading logic (buy near artificial ceiling, sell on breakout) can be approximated using price proximity to a user-supplied ceiling parameter and upward momentum (close > prev_close), but the fundamental requirement to identify and validate the existence of an enforceable artificial price floor/ceiling cannot be operationalized with standard OHLCV indicators. This signal type requires external data feeds (regulatory announcements, price control registries) or manual event detection.
